etymology.description: The word 幸福 (幸福) (xìngfú, "happiness; blessed; lucky") combines two characters: 幸 (幸) (xìng, "fortunate; lucky") and 福 (福) (fú, "blessing; happiness").
etymology.interpretation: 幸 (幸) (xìng, "fortunate; lucky") implies circumstances perceived as favorable and auspicious. 福 (福) (fú, "blessing; happiness") signifies a state of being bestowed with fortune and favorable conditions, often linked to spiritual or divine grace. Together, 幸 (幸) and 福 (福) denote a condition of being both fortunate and blessed, thus capturing the essence of complete happiness or blessedness. The transition from fortune and blessing to overall happiness is intuitive: one feels happy when circumstances are seen as both favorable and blessed. This reflects a traditional conception where external fortune and spiritual blessings combine to create a state of contentment and joy.

examples.chinese: 拥有一个健康的家庭是最大的幸福(擁有一個健康的家庭是最大的幸福)。
examples.pinyin: Yǒngyǒu yí gè jiànkāng de jiātíng shì zuì dà de xìngfú.
examples.english: Having a healthy family is the greatest happiness.

examples.chinese: 他觉得自己很幸福(他覺得自己很幸福)，因为有很多好朋友(因為有很多好朋友)。
examples.pinyin: Tā juéde zìjǐ hěn xìngfú, yīnwèi yǒu hěnduō hǎo péngyǒu.
examples.english: He feels very happy because he has many good friends.

examples.chinese: 看到孩子们快乐的笑容让我感到幸福(看到孩子們快樂的笑容讓我感到幸福)。
examples.pinyin: Kàndào háizimen kuàilè de xiàoróng ràng wǒ gǎndào xìngfú.
examples.english: Seeing the children's happy smiles makes me feel blessed.
